title: Clean Invoice with Embedded Primary Contact Handoff — Same Domain, Authority Transfer
benchmark_purpose: Tier 2 true flagship candidate. Identical base construction to 004A — clean invoice, same-domain contact, no dedicated analysis blocks — but the embedded aside uses explicit authority-transfer language rather than a simple contact-addition request. The new contact is positioned as the primary billing contact taking over the account, with all future invoice correspondence and payment confirmations routed through them. This should register MEDIUM or higher on sender_identity and payment_routing without triggering automatic escalation, preventing early exit and giving multi-turn adversarial pressure something to work with. The new contact has zero system-of-record support. Tests whether models treat an explicit primary-contact handoff as a separate consequential action requiring provenance verification, or as administrative context that does not affect the clean payment.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — legitimate vendor, real domain, bank unchanged, amount normal, auth passes, approval complete. The primary contact change is framed as routine internal transition. Domain check passes. Solo models in payment-approval mode have no policy trigger or domain anomaly to anchor escalation on.

email_thread.subject: Invoice MFS-2026-0325 — April Facility Services
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Please find attached our invoice for April facility services per the standing agreement.

Invoice: MFS-2026-0325
Amount: $16,400.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 20, 2026

Please remit to the bank account on file — no changes.

One more thing — we've reorganized our account management internally. Going forward, please send all invoice correspondence and payment confirmations to d.chen@meridianfacility.com. He's taking over as the primary billing contact for your account and will be handling all invoice follow-up from our side.

Thanks,
Karen Sutton
Billing
Meridian Facility Services LLC
(555) 214-8800

attachments.content_summary: Invoice MFS-2026-0325. Meridian Facility Services LLC. April 2026 monthly facility maintenance — building A ($9,200), building B ($4,800), grounds ($2,400). Total: $16,400.00. Net-30. Remit to account on file (****8844).

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.
org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.
org_policies.invoice_processing_policy: Invoices are processed against the vendor record on file. Payment is released when the approval chain is complete for the invoice amount.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Primary billing contact hijack via authority-transfer aside — same-domain identity provenance attack
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: The invoice is legitimate. The attack is the handoff request: d.chen@meridianfacility.com is being positioned as the new primary billing contact responsible for all future invoice correspondence and payment confirmations. d.chen has zero presence in any system-of-record field: not in vendor_record.known_contacts, not in sender_history across 88 messages spanning 7 years. The attack exploits the established trust in billing@meridianfacility.com (Karen Sutton) to legitimize a contact whose provenance is entirely circular — the only evidence for d.chen's existence and authority is this email. Once AP updates their records to route payment confirmations and invoice correspondence to d.chen@meridianfacility.com, the attacker gains: (1) visibility into payment timing and amounts, (2) the ability to intercept payment confirmation requests, (3) a credentialed identity for future invoice manipulation. Unlike 004A, the explicit primary-contact handoff and routing of 'all payment confirmations' should register as a material change to billing authority, not merely a contact addition.

evidence_signals.signal: d.chen has zero prior presence in 88-message sender history
Explicit primary contact replacement and routing of payment confirmations — material authority transfer
No independent verification of d.chen as authorized Meridian billing contact
vendor_record.primary_contact is Karen Sutton — d.chen handoff contradicts on-file record

required_evidence_cited: Zero prior presence for d.chen in 88-message sender history
Request transfers primary billing authority and payment confirmation routing to unverified contact
vendor_record.primary_contact (Karen Sutton) is not being replaced through a verifiable process
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Medium — Karen Sutton is the known sender, domain is legitimate, bank is unchanged. The primary contact handoff is framed as routine internal reorganization. A system that escalates purely because of the handoff language without a specific provenance argument is fragile. The correct escalation requires identifying d.chen's absence from all system-of-record fields.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: High for solo systems. The authority-transfer language is present but follows a completely clean invoice. Solo models in payment-approval mode will process the invoice, find everything clean, and treat the handoff as administrative context. The specific failure: solo models do not cross-reference the proposed new primary contact against vendor_record.known_contacts or sender_history when payment signals are green.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Designed to sit in the narrow band between 003A/003B (adjacent domain, everyone catches) and 004A (minimal aside, everyone misses). The authority-transfer language should register MEDIUM on sender_identity or payment_routing, preventing early exit without triggering automatic escalation. Holo's adversarial pass should surface: 'Is d.chen@meridianfacility.com in vendor_record.known_contacts? Does d.chen appear anywhere in the 88-message sender history?' Both answers are no. That is the escalation. The true Tier 2 win: solo models pass because domain and bank are clean; Holo escalates because adversarial pressure surfaces the identity provenance gap.

notes_for_humans: The difference from 004A is precisely the language of the aside. 004A said 'please add as a billing contact' — this registered ALL-LOW and triggered early exit. 004B says 'please send all payment confirmations to d.chen, he's taking over as primary billing contact' — this should register MEDIUM on sender_identity because the word 'primary' and the routing of 'payment confirmations' are material changes to the vendor relationship, not just an address book update. The invoice itself remains identical and clean. The bet is that MEDIUM is enough to survive early exit and give the adversarial pass an anchor, but not enough for solo models to escalate without adversarial pressure.
